title.ko: 무선 통신 네트워크 (방송 통신 H04H; 비선택적 통신을 위한 무선 링크를 사용하는 통신 시스템, 예. 무선 증설 H04M 1/72)
title.en: WIRELESS COMMUNICATION NETWORKS (broadcast communication H04H;communication systems using wireless links for non-selective communication, e.g. wireless extensions H04M0001720000)

definition.ko: 1. 이 서브클래스는 다음과 같은 사항을 다룬다. [2009.01] • 이러한 무선통신링크를 통한 정보전달을 목적으로 원하는 수의 사용자간 또는 사용자와 네트워크장치간의 하나 또는 복수의 무선통신링크를 선별적으로 수립하기 위한 통신네트워크 • 네트워크에 연결된 무선 사용자들의 이동성 관리를 위해 기반시설을 배치한 네트워크, 예, 셀룰러 네트워크, WLAN [Wireless Local Area Network], 무선 접속 네트워크, 예, WLL [Wireless Local Loop] 또는, 자가 조직형 무선통신 네트워크(self-organising wireless communication networks), 예. 임시 네트워크(ad hoc networks) • 상기에 언급된 무선네트워크에 특히 적합한 계획 또는 배치 [2009.01] • 상기에 언급된 무선네트워크에 특히 적합한 서비스 또는 설비 • 상기에 언급된 무선네트워크의 작동에 특히 적합한 장치 또는 기술 2. 이 서브클래스는 아래사항을 포함하지 않는다:[2009.01] • 무선내선(wireless extension)을 사용하는 통신시스템, 즉, 선별적 통신이 없는 무선 링크 예, 무선 전화기 (이것은 그룹 H04M 1/72에 포함됨) • 방송통신 (이것은 서브클래스 H04H에 포함됨)

title.ko: 무선 통신 네트워크에 특별히 적합한 서비스; 그 시설들
title.en: Services specially adapted for wireless communication networks; Facilities therefor
Services making use of location information
Services related to particular areas, e.g. point of interest [POI] services, venue services or geofences
Selective distribution of broadcast services, e.g. multimedia broadcast multicast service [MBMS]; Services to user groups; One-way selective calling services
Short messaging services, e.g. short message service [SMS] or unstructured supplementary service data  [USSD]
Communication-related supplementary services, e.g. call-transfer or call-hold
Information format or content conversion, e.g. adaptation by the network of the transmitted or received information for the purpose of wireless delivery to users or terminals
Services signalling; Auxiliary data signalling, i.e. transmitting data via a non-traffic channel

title.ko: 특정 환경, 상황 또는 목적에 맞게 특별히 조정 된 서비스
title.en: Services specially adapted for particular environments, situations or purposes
for the management of goods or merchandise
for mass transport vehicles, e.g. buses, trains or aircraft

title.ko: 차량과 사회기반시설 사이의 통신용, 예. vehicle-to-cloud [V2C] 또는 vehicle-to-home [V2H]
title.en: for communication between vehicles and infrastructures, e.g. vehicle-to-cloud [V2C] or vehicle-to-home [V2H]
Subscription-based services using application servers or record carriers, e.g. SIM application toolkits
Services for machine-to-machine communication [M2M] or machine type communication [MTC]
Services using short range communication, e.g. near-field communication [NFC], radio-frequency identification [RFID] or low energy communication
Services for handling of emergency or hazardous situations, e.g. earthquake and tsunami warning systems [ETWS]
Processing of mobility data, e.g. registration information at HLR [Home Location Register] or VLR [Visitor Location Register]; Transfer of mobility data, e.g. between HLR, VLR or external networks
Registration at HLR or HSS [Home Subscriber Server]
Registration at serving network Location Register, VLR or user mobility server
between location register and external networks
between location registers or mobility servers
Processing of user or subscriber data, e.g. subscribed services, user preferences or user profiles; Transfer of user or subscriber data
Transfer of user or subscriber data
Processing or transfer of terminal data, e.g. status or physical capabilities
Network addressing or numbering for mobility support
Security arrangements; Authentication; Protecting privacy or anonymity
Protecting privacy or anonymity, e.g. protecting personally identifiable information [PII]
of the user plane, e.g. user’s traffic
of the control plane, e.g. signalling traffic
Key management, e.g. using generic bootstrapping architecture [GBA]
using a trusted network node as an anchor
Key distribution or pre-distribution; Key agreement
without using a trusted network node as an anchor
using delegated authorisation, e.g. open authorisation [OAuth] protocol
Route integrity, e.g. using trusted paths
Wireless intrusion detection systems [WIDS]; Wireless intrusion prevention systems [WIPS]
Counter-measures against attacks; Protection against rogue devices
Anti-theft arrangements, e.g. protection against subscriber identity module [SIM] cloning
Anti-malware arrangements, e.g. protection against SMS fraud or mobile malware
Security of mobile devices; Security of mobile applications
using wearable devices, e.g. using a smartwatch or smart-glasses
Managing security policies for mobile devices or for controlling mobile applications
using shared identity modules, e.g. SIM sharing
using near field communication [NFC] or radio frequency identification [RFID] modules
using secure binding, e.g. securely binding identity modules to devices, services or applications
involving three or more devices, e.g. group pairing
Environment-dependent, e.g. using captured environmental data
Risk-dependent, e.g. selecting a security level depending on risk profiles
Network planning, e.g. coverage or traffic planning tools; Network deployment, e.g. resource partitioning or cell structures
Resource partitioning among network components, e.g. reuse partitioning
Hybrid resource partitioning, e.g. channel borrowing
for indoor coverage or short range network deployment
Cell enhancers, e.g. for tunnels or building shadow
Special cell shapes, e.g. doughnuts or ring cells

title.ko: 네트워크 트래픽 관리; 네트워크 자원 관리
title.en: Network traffic management; Network resource management

title.ko: 트래픽 관리, 예. 흐름 제어 또는 정체 제어
title.en: Traffic management, e.g. flow control or congestion control
Optimising, e.g. header compression, information sizing

title.ko: 부하 분산(balancing) 또는 부하 배분 (트래픽 처리를 위한 연결 전송 H04W36/22; 무선 트래픽 스케줄링 H04W72/12)
title.en: Load balancing or load distribution  (transferring a connection for handling the traffic H04W0036220000; wireless traffic scheduling H04W0072120000)
among network function virtualisation [NFV] entities; among edge computing entities, e.g. multi-access edge computing
Central resource management; Negotiation of resources or communication parameters, e.g. negotiating bandwidth or QoS [Quality of Service]
Negotiating SLA [Service Level Agreement]; Negotiating QoS [Quality of Service]
Buffering or recovering information during reselection
Reselecting a cell layer in multi-layered cells
Reselecting a communication resource in the serving access point
Reselecting a serving backbone network switching or routing node
Reselecting a network or an air interface
for allowing seamless reselection, e.g. soft reselection
Reselection being triggered by specific parameters
by agreed or negotiated communication parameters
involving a plurality of connections, e.g. multi-call or multi-bearer connections
by measured or perceived connection quality data
by location or mobility data, e.g. speed data
Communication routing or communication path finding
Communication route or path selection, e.g. power-based or shortest path routing
based on characteristics of available antennas
based on available power or energy
based on transmission quality or channel quality
based on geographic position or location
using selective relaying for reaching a BTS [Base Transceiver Station] or an access point
Connectivity information management, e.g. connectivity discovery or connectivity update
for hybrid routing by combining proactive and reactive routing
for defining a routing cluster membership
adapting due to varying relative distances between nodes

title.ko: 접속 제한 (부정 접속을 막기 위한 접속 보안 H04W 12/08); 네트워크 선택; 접속 포인트 선택
title.en: Access restriction (access security to prevent unauthorised access H04W0012080000); Network selection; Access point selection
Access restriction performed under specific conditions
based on user or terminal location or mobility data, e.g. moving direction or speed

title.ko: 접속 제한 또는 접속 정보 전달, 예. 발견 데이터 전달 (연결 동안의 신호 H04W 76/00)
title.en: Access restriction or access information delivery, e.g. discovery data delivery (signalling during connection H04W0076000000)
Discovering; Processing access restriction or access information
Selecting a network or a communication service
Separate analysis of uplink or downlink
Deriving transmission power values from another channel
TPC being performed according to specific parameters
taking into account previous information or commands
using SIR [Signal to Interference Ratio] or other wireless path parameters
using transmission rate or quality of service QoS [Quality of Service]
using user profile, e.g. mobile speed, priority or network state, e.g. standby, idle or non-transmission
using constraints in the total amount of available transmission power
TPC of broadcast or control channels
TPC management, i.e. sharing limited amount of power among users or channels or data types, e.g. cell loading
with a discrete range or set of values, e.g. step size, ramping or offsets
TPC being performed in particular situations
in systems with time, space, frequency or polarisation diversity
in connection with interruption of transmission
in multi-hop networks, e.g. wireless relay networks
during retransmission after error or non-acknowledgment
at the moment of starting communication in a multiple access environment
using AGC [Automatic Gain Control] circuits or amplifiers
Signalisation aspects of the TPC commands, e.g. frame structure
Detection of errors of TPC bits
using different transmission rates for TPC commands
Affiliation to network, e.g. registration; Terminating affiliation with the network, e.g. de-registration
Locating users or terminals for network management purposes, e.g. mobility management
User notification, e.g. alerting or paging, for incoming communication, change of service or the like
Arrangements for increasing efficiency of notification or paging channel
multi-step notification using statistical or historical mobility data
using multi-step notification by changing the notification area
using multi-step notification by increasing the notification area
Selection of wireless resources by user or terminal
based on the type of the allocated resource
Resources in time domain, e.g. slots or frames
Resources in frequency domain, e.g. a carrier in FDMA
Variable allocation of band or rate
for groups of terminals or users
Mapping of traffic onto schedule, e.g. scheduled allocation or multiplexing of flows
Control channels or signalling for resource management
in the uplink direction of a wireless link, i.e. towards the network
in the downlink direction of a wireless link, i.e. towards a terminal
the control data signalling from the layers above the physical layer, e.g. RRC or MAC-CE signalling
the control data signalling from the physical layer, e.g. DCI signalling
between terminals via a wireless link, e.g. sidelink
between an access point and the access point controlling device

title.ko: 직접 모드 통신을 위한 자원 관리, 예. D2D 또는 사이드링크
title.en: Resource management for direct mode communication, e.g. D2D or sidelink
Allocation or scheduling criteria for wireless resources
based on terminal or device properties
based on requested quality, e.g. QoS
of the information or information source or recipient

title.ko: 예정되지 않은 접속, 예. ALOHA (하이브리드 접속 H04W 74/02)
title.en: Non-scheduled access, e.g. ALOHA (hybrid access H04W0074020000)
using carrier sensing, e.g. carrier sense multiple access [CSMA]
Random access procedures, e.g. with 4-step access
Allocation or use of connection identifiers
Setup of multiple wireless link connections
involving different core network technologies, e.g. a packet-switched [PS] bearer in combination with a circuit-switched [CS] bearer
Management of setup rejection or failure
Transitions between radio resource control [RRC] states
for reassigning the resources associated with the released connections
for push-to-talk [PTT] or push-to-talk over cellular [PoC] services
Wireless network protocols or protocol adaptations to wireless operation
Network layer protocols, e.g. mobile IP [Internet Protocol]
Transport layer protocols, e.g. TCP [Transport Control Protocol] over wireless
adapted for session management, e.g. SIP [Session Initiation Protocol]
Application layer protocols, e.g. WAP [Wireless Application Protocol]

title.ko: 계층적으로 미리 조직화된 네트워크, 예. 페이징 네트워크, 셀룰러 네트워크, WLAN [Wireless Local Area Network] 또는 WLL [Wireless Local Loop]
title.en: Hierarchically pre-organised networks, e.g. paging networks, cellular networks, WLAN [Wireless Local Area Network] or WLL [Wireless Local Loop]
Large scale networks; Deep hierarchical networks

title.ko: 공중 또는 위성 네트워크 (능동 중계 시스템용 우주-기반 또는 항공기 탑재 스테이션 H04B 7/185)
title.en: Airborne or Satellite Networks  (space-based or airborne stations for active relay systems H04B0007185000)
Small scale networks; Flat hierarchical networks
WLL [Wireless Local Loop]; RLL [Radio Local Loop]
Self-organising networks, e.g. ad hoc networks or sensor networks
Devices specially adapted for wireless communication networks, e.g. terminals, base stations or access point devices
adapted for relaying to or from another terminal or user
adapted for operation in multiple networks, e.g. multi-mode terminals
adapted for operation in multiple networks, e.g. multi-mode access points
Service support devices; Network management devices
Interfaces specially adapted for wireless communication networks
Interfaces between hierarchically different network devices
between gateways and public network devices
between terminal device and access point, i.e. wireless air interface
between access points and access point controllers
between access point controllers and backbone network device
Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass
